Caption: (a) Schematic of the building block of lithium-ion batteries, (b) CT scan of a cylindrical cell, (c) CT scan of a pouch cell, exhibiting their internal layered structures.

(a) In-plane confined test setup for bare pouch cell. Sixteen half wave buckles were formed in the cell interior under in-plane compression, (b) comparison of stress–strain curves from confined in-plane and unconfined through-thickness compression tests (Sahraei et al., 2012b), (c) schematic of a plate subjected to in-plane compressive loads in two perpendicular directions. The plate is constrained by an elastic foundation in the lateral direction (z-axis).

Effects of cell length (top), cell thickness (middle), and cell elastic modulus (bottom) on cell buckling stress (left) and buckling wavelength (right). ▴ represents the values calculated from reference dimensions: L = 0 . 03 m, b = 0 . 05 m, and h = 0 . 0053 m.

(a) The finite element model of the representative unit (RU). (b) Deformation patterns observed in the case of in-plane loading of an RU with length shorter than (left) and equal to (right) one buckling wavelength, λ .

Predicted deformation modes of a pouch cell under in-plane compression predicted by (a) the RVE model and (b) the Full-Thickness model. (c) Comparison of values of buckling stress from implicit FE simulation using 1 RU, RVE, and Full Thickness models.

(a) Hardening curves in MD, TD, TTD, and shear directions (logarithmic strain values are used) with Macro FE model of the small pouch cell, (b) comparison of load–displacement curves from in-plane compression tests in the width direction with simulation results.

(a) Elliptical cell and its components used in finite element model and the deformed cell under in-plane loading, (b) comparison of load–displacement curves between simulation and in-plane compression tests (Kermani et al., 2019) on elliptical cells.
Comparison of pouch and elliptical cells’ RVE models.
